steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage explicitly states that sellers “profited from those customers' passivity in their interim” — showing that consumer inaction is what benefits retailers. The blank asks what consumer quality makes this beneficial, so we need a word for the tendency to not actively cancel subscriptions.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
Inertia directly captures what the passage calls “passivity” — the tendency not to take action, allowing consumers to keep automatically paying despite no longer valuing the subscription. The other choices either describe active behavior (decisiveness or evasion) or reference business metrics (turnover), none explaining why passive consumers benefit retailers. Inertia — the reluctance to change — is precisely what keeps subscribers paying and benefits retailers.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
